notes.gen_sg: Both 'mě' and 'mne' are standard in the genitive, both with and without prepositions (e.g. 'od mě'/'od mne', 'bez mě'/'bez mne'). 'Mne' is more emphatic/formal; 'mě' is neutral.
notes.dat_sg: 'Mi' is the enclitic used without prepositions. 'Mně' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'ke mně') and for emphasis.
notes.acc_sg: 'Mě' is the most common form, used both after prepositions (e.g. 'pro mě', 'na mě') and as an enclitic. 'Mne' is the stressed/emphatic variant (e.g. 'na mne'). Note: 'mně' is dative/locative — using it in the accusative is a common mistake.
notes.loc_sg: Only 'mně' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 'o mně', 'po mně'). There is no enclitic form.
notes.ins_sg: Only 'mnou' exists (e.g. 'se mnou', 'přede mnou'). There is no enclitic form.

notes.gen_sg: 'Tě' is the enclitic used without prepositions. 'Tebe' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'od tebe', 'bez tebe') and for emphasis.
notes.dat_sg: 'Ti' is the enclitic used without prepositions. 'Tobě' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'k tobě') and for emphasis.
notes.acc_sg: 'Tě' is the enclitic used without prepositions. 'Tebe' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'na tebe', 'pro tebe') and for emphasis.
notes.loc_sg: Only 'tobě' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 'o tobě', 'po tobě'). There is no enclitic form.
notes.ins_sg: Only 'tebou' exists (e.g. 's tebou', 'před tebou'). There is no enclitic form.

notes.gen_sg: After prepositions, use n-forms: 'od něho', 'bez něj'. Without prepositions: 'ho' (enclitic) or 'jeho' (emphatic). Never use n-forms without a preposition or j-forms after a preposition.
notes.dat_sg: After prepositions, use 'němu' (e.g. 'k němu'). Without prepositions: 'mu' (enclitic) or 'jemu' (emphatic).
notes.acc_sg: After prepositions, use n-forms: 'na něho', 'pro něj'. Without prepositions: 'ho' (enclitic). The form 'jeho' in accusative is archaic.
notes.loc_sg: Only 'něm' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 'o něm', 'po něm').
notes.ins_sg: Only 'ním' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 's ním', 'před ním').

notes.gen_sg: 'Ní' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'od ní', 'bez ní'). 'Jí' is the enclitic used without prepositions.
notes.dat_sg: 'Ní' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'k ní'). 'Jí' is the enclitic used without prepositions.
notes.acc_sg: 'Ni' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'na ni', 'pro ni'). 'Ji' is the enclitic used without prepositions. Note the vowel difference: gen/dat 'ní/jí' vs acc 'ni/ji'.
notes.loc_sg: Only 'ní' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 'o ní', 'po ní'). There is no enclitic form.
notes.ins_sg: Only 'ní' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 's ní', 'před ní'). There is no enclitic form.

notes.gen_sg: After prepositions, use n-forms: 'od něho', 'bez něj'. Without prepositions: 'ho' (enclitic) or 'jeho' (emphatic).
notes.dat_sg: After prepositions, use 'němu' (e.g. 'k němu'). Without prepositions: 'mu' (enclitic) or 'jemu' (emphatic).
notes.acc_sg: After prepositions, use n-forms: 'na ně', 'pro něj'. Without prepositions, the standard neuter form is 'je'. Note: neuter 'ně'/'je' differs from masculine 'něho'/'ho'.
notes.loc_sg: Only 'něm' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 'o něm').
notes.ins_sg: Only 'ním' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 's ním').

notes.general: 'My' has no distinct enclitic forms. The same forms are used in all positions, including after prepositions (e.g. 'od nás', 'k nám', 's námi').

'Vy' has no distinct enclitic forms. The same forms are used in all positions, including after prepositions (e.g. 'od vás', 'k vám', 's vámi'). When used as a polite form of address, it is capitalized: Vy, Vás, Vám, etc.

notes.nom_pl: Nominative varies by gender: 'oni' (masculine animate), 'ony' (masculine inanimate and feminine), 'ona' (neuter). The lemma uses 'oni' as the default masculine animate form.
notes.gen_pl: After prepositions, use n-form 'nich' (e.g. 'od nich', 'bez nich'). Without prepositions, use 'jich' (both enclitic and emphatic).
notes.dat_pl: After prepositions, use n-form 'nim' (e.g. 'k nim'). Without prepositions, use 'jim' (both enclitic and emphatic).
notes.acc_pl: After prepositions, use n-form 'ně' (e.g. 'na ně', 'pro ně'). Without prepositions, use 'je' (both enclitic and emphatic).
notes.loc_pl: Only 'nich' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 'o nich').
notes.ins_pl: Only 'nimi' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 's nimi').

notes.general: The reflexive pronoun has no nominative form and no plural. It refers back to the subject regardless of person, number, or gender.
notes.dat_sg: 'Si' is the enclitic used without prepositions (e.g. 'koupil si knihu'). 'Sobě' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'k sobě') and for emphasis.
notes.acc_sg: 'Se' is the enclitic used without prepositions (e.g. 'mýt se'). 'Sebe' is used after prepositions (e.g. 'na sebe', 'pro sebe') and for emphasis.
notes.loc_sg: Only 'sobě' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 'o sobě', 'v sobě'). There is no enclitic form.
notes.ins_sg: Only 'sebou' exists; always used after prepositions (e.g. 'se sebou', 'před sebou'). There is no enclitic form.
